findings.description: Repeated header and footer content from the PDF pages is rendered inline in the HTML between content sections, disrupting reading flow.
findings.evidence: In the PDF, the header block (ROY COOPER Governor, ELIZABETH S. BISER Secretary, KATHY B. RAWLS Director) and footer block (State of North Carolina | Division of Marine Fisheries | address) appear only as page-level elements. In the HTML, these are inserted inline between the Gear Restrictions section and the IV. General Information section, forcing a reader to pass through repeated administrative header/footer text mid-document.

findings.description: A full-page-width image (612pt, the width of a letter-size page) is inserted into the HTML body between Section III (Gear Restrictions) and Section IV (General Information), corresponding to the PDF page 1/page 2 break. This is a page-break artifact (likely a captured page footer/separator) dropped into the body content flow, breaking the reading flow between sections.
findings.evidence:
HTML contains: directly before

IV. GENERAL INFORMATION

. No corresponding substantive image exists at this location in the PDF; it aligns with the page 1 to page 2 transition.
